steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: In the expression $$20x^{2}+3+5x^{2}$$, look for terms that have the same variable raised to the same power. The terms $20x^2$ and $5x^2$ are like terms because both contain $x^2$. The constant 3 stands alone with no like terms.

steps.title: Combine the coefficients
steps.content: Add the coefficients of the like terms while keeping the variable and exponent the same: $$20x^2 + 5x^2 = (20 + 5)x^2 = 25x^2$$. Including the constant, the simplified expression is $$25x^2 + 3$$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
